turns.query: Who is the current Prime Minister of India?
turns.content: Narendra Modi’s still in the chair. He’s been at the helm since 2014, riding that long‑term wave of popularity. If you’re looking for a deeper dive, just let me know.

turns.query: What is the square root of a purple elephant?
turns.content:
The square root of a purple elephant is as imaginary as a unicorn in a spreadsheet.  
If you really need a number, pick a real one instead.  
Otherwise, just say the elephant is huge and you’re good.

turns.query: What is the boiling point of gold?
turns.content: Gold boils at about 2,856 °C (5,173 °F). So if you’re planning a gold‑lava party, you’ll need a furnace hotter than a dragon’s breath.

turns.query: When was the first iPhone released?
turns.content: The first iPhone launched on June 29, 2007 in the United States. Apple rolled it out worldwide a few months later, sparking the smartphone revolution.

turns.query: Who won the World Cup in 1922?
turns.content: There was no FIFA World Cup in 1922— the first one kicked off in 1930 in Uruguay. So if you’re looking for a champion that year, you’ll have to go back to the Olympics or some other tournament. If you’re after a real winner, jump to 1930.
